title: Sound Natural Without Sounding Too Casual
description: 今天练的是工作和朋友聊天都能用的柔和推进：不催、不硬，但能把事情往前带。

items.phrase: Just to make sure we're aligned...
items.cn: 用来确认双方理解一致。比直接说 “Do you understand?” 礼貌很多，也更像真实会议用语。
examples: Just to make sure we're aligned, the first draft is due this Friday, right?
Just to make sure we're aligned, we're prioritizing speed over polish for this version.
Just to make sure we're aligned, I'll handle the slides and you'll send over the numbers.
dialogue: A
Just to make sure we're aligned, we're not launching this week, right?
B
Right. We're aiming for next Tuesday now.
A
Got it. I'll update the timeline.

items.cn: 字面是“没有压力”，实际常用来温和提醒或邀请。朋友、同事都能用，但后面不要接特别强硬的要求。
examples: No pressure, but if you have time today, could you take a quick look?
No pressure, but we're grabbing coffee downstairs if you want to join.
No pressure, but I think you'd really like this place.
dialogue: A
No pressure, but we're doing dinner around seven if you're free.
B
That actually sounds nice. Where are you going?
A
A Thai place near the office.

items.cn: 意思是“我不是非坚持这个方案不可”。很适合提方案时降低压迫感。
examples: I put together a rough outline, but I'm not married to it.
We could lead with the pricing slide, but I'm not married to it.
I'm not married to this wording, so feel free to tweak it.
dialogue: A
I moved the customer story to the beginning. I'm not married to it, though.
B
I actually like it there. It makes the deck feel more concrete.
A
Great, let's keep it for now.

items.cn: 意思是“说得通 / 符合逻辑 / 难怪”。朋友聊天很常见，工作里熟悉的同事之间也可以用。
examples: You only slept four hours? That tracks.
They moved the deadline again? Yeah, that tracks.
You liked the quiet cafe more than the fancy one? That tracks.
dialogue: A
I thought I'd hate the networking event, but it was actually useful.
B
That tracks. You're good in small groups.
A
Apparently I just hate loud rooms.
